text:
Most PLG companies think they're running Product-Led Sales.

They're actually running Product-Led Lead Scoring with a sales team bolted on. And it's costing them 40-60% of expansion revenue.

Here's the pattern I keep seeing:

1. Product team ships usage analytics
2. RevOps defines a PQL based on generic thresholds (logins, seats, feature adoption)
3. PQLs get routed to AEs carrying six-figure quotas
4. Reps call 3-5 days later, treating the signal like an MQL
5. The user has already mentally churned or self-served their way past the moment

The economics don't work. A $100K-quota AE can't profitably chase a 12-seat expansion. And usage thresholds without context (collaboration depth, integration breadth, team activation patterns) generate noise, not intent.

Benchmark data is clear: PLS companies outgrow sales-led peers by ~50%, but only when reps engage inside 24 hours. After 72, conversion drops by more than half.

My 12-24 month prediction:

PLS will split into its own GTM discipline with a dedicated rep tier ($40-60K OTE, high-velocity, product-fluent), distinct PQL taxonomies per product surface, and real-time triggers wired into the workflow rather than nightly Salesforce syncs. The companies still treating PLS as "PLG + AEs" will lose the expansion game to ones who built the motion natively.

For PMs: stop shipping usage dashboards. Start defining the 3-5 behavioral patterns that signal a buying conversation, not just engagement.

For leaders: audit your PQL-to-touch SLA. If it's over 24 hours, you're funding a leaky bucket.

What's your current PQL-to-conversation time?

#PLS #PLG #SaaS
